source: BAT-761 migration from v1 (BAT-699 → PR #378 R10) + BAT-706 audit_pending population

mpp_protocol.label: Multi-Party Protocol (MPP)
mpp_protocol.explanation: Service uses pay.sh's newer Multi-Party Protocol settlement flow. Our X402Protocol implementation only supports the standard x402 v2 single-signer flow. Adding MPP support is non-trivial — separate BAT, not yet filed.

siwx_auth_required.label: Sign-In-With-X auth required
siwx_auth_required.explanation: Service requires a wallet auth handshake (sign-in-with-Solana style) before it will return a 402 with payment requirements. We don't implement that auth pre-flight yet. Tracked-adjacent in BAT-697 (Trigger V2 also needs SIWX) — once SIWX lands there, these become payable.

invalid_demand.label: Service returns 402 with amount=0 (no demand)
invalid_demand.explanation: Service returns HTTP 402 with amount=0 — semantically 'free' from x402's perspective, but still a 402 status. agent_pay refuses zero-demand by design, AND our web_fetch tool throws on any non-2xx response (so it can't fetch these either). Net result: not currently usable via our tools. Possible follow-up: a tool that fetches with 402-tolerant semantics, or a flag on web_fetch to allow 402 pass-through.

requires_binary_response.label: Service expected to return binary content (image/audio/video) that we can't deliver
requires_binary_response.explanation: agent_pay parses x402 settles and returns the response body as best-effort UTF-8 text. Services expected to return binary content (PNG screenshots, AI-generated images, audio, video) can be paid but the binary bytes can't currently be piped to the Telegram/Discord channel as an attachment — there's no in-memory-buffer-to-channel path. Tracked: needs a follow-up BAT to (a) save binary content to a workspace file and (b) plumb that file path to the channel sender. Each service's `note` field documents the EVIDENCE BASIS for the classification: 'paid-response-observed' (we have a captured paid response confirming binary), 'product-family-inference' (the service's published product is image/audio/video gen — e.g. fal.ai Flux/SDXL/Stable-Video — making binary the only plausible response shape, AND no openapi evidence contradicts it), or 'openapi-response-content-type' (the openapi spec declares image/* / video/* / audio/* media types). Distinct from `unverified_paid_response_shape`: a service goes there if the evidence is contested (e.g. openapi declares application/json while product family suggests binary), as happened with paysponge/screenshotone in PR #378 R10. Operational consequence: refuse the service to avoid spending USDC for output the channel can't deliver.

endpoint_not_402_at_probe.label: Service exists in pay.sh's catalog but didn't return a 402 at probe time
endpoint_not_402_at_probe.explanation: Service is listed in pay.sh's upstream catalog but our probe got a non-402 HTTP status (4xx/5xx, sometimes 200/301). Possible causes: endpoint moved, broken, auth-gated differently, or re-routed to a free response. agent_pay needs a 402 to settle, so it can't pay these. web_fetch could in theory reach a 2xx response, but the agent doesn't know the request shape and shouldn't probe blindly. Each entry's `note` field records the probe-time HTTP status as a hint. Tell the user the service is listed upstream but not currently reachable through our tooling. (Note: for some entries the BAT-706 audit later found OTHER payable endpoints on the SAME host that the catalog URL didn't point at — see the per-entry note.)

unverified_paid_response_shape.label: Paid endpoint exists and parses OK, but the response shape isn't yet verified — we can't promise the channel can render the output
unverified_paid_response_shape.explanation: agent_pay would settle (we have a parseable 402 with Solana-USDC), but we have NOT captured an actual paid response, and the available evidence is contested (e.g. openapi declares one content-type while product-family inference suggests another). Conservative refuse: a paid call could burn USDC on output the channel can't render (binary bytes with no file-pipe path), OR could succeed with JSON whose envelope shape we haven't documented. Different from `requires_binary_response`, which we only assert when evidence clearly points to binary content (paid-response-observed or unambiguous openapi media types). Deferred to BAT-708 for paid-response capture + classification before catalog inclusion.

entries.name: paysponge/screenshotone
entries.note: ScreenshotOne is a screenshot-as-a-service product (publicly documented at screenshotone.com); PRODUCT-FAMILY INFERENCE alone suggested binary content (PNG/GIF/JPEG/MP4) on /animate and /take. HOWEVER, the captured 402 openapi for the catalog endpoint DECLARES application/json — see tests/paysh/captures/catalog/paysponge-screenshotone.json (resource.mimeType: "application/json", bazaar output.type: "json"). That CONTRADICTS the binary-by-family inference: the paid response could be a URL- or base64-wrapped JSON envelope we CAN deliver to a channel, or a JSON shape we haven't documented. We have NOT settled a paid call to observe the actual response body. Conservative refuse — deferred to BAT-708 for paid-response capture before catalog inclusion. Updated 2026-05-15 (PR #378 R10) to RETRACT the earlier requires_binary_response classification, which rested on family inference and didn't reconcile with the openapi evidence.

entries.name: paysponge/coingecko
entries.note: http_404 at 2026-05-14 probe — endpoint removed; replaced upstream by stablecrypto/market-data which is in catalog.json.

entries.name: paysponge/fal
entries.note: BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15) confirmed 9 paid endpoints on fal.x402.paysponge.com all return parseable 402 challenges for Solana-USDC: /fal-ai/fast-sdxl ($0.01), /fal-ai/flux/dev ($0.03), /fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1 ($0.04), /fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1-ultra ($0.06), /fal-ai/flux/schnell ($0.01), /fal-ai/minimax/video-01 ($0.07), /fal-ai/recraft-v3 ($0.04), /fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v35-large ($0.04), /fal-ai/stable-video ($0.07). Evidence basis for the binary classification: PRODUCT-FAMILY INFERENCE — the endpoint names map to fal-ai's published image- and video-generation models (Flux SDXL, Recraft v3, Stable Diffusion v3.5, Stable Video, Minimax-video) which return PNG/MP4 binary content by their nature. The audit confirms the 402-and-build path works; we have NOT settled a payment and inspected the response body. Follow-up path to upgrade evidence_basis: extract declared response content-types from fal's openapi.json (the audit's fetcher reaches it successfully — see catalog-audit.md enumerating 30 endpoints from it; WebFetch with a different user-agent gets HTTP 403, but probe-catalog.js's UA works). Parsing openapi response.content media types per endpoint would let us flip this to evidence_basis: openapi-response-content-type. Until then we refuse to avoid spending USDC on output the channel can't deliver. See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md for the parsed_ok evidence (run 2026-05-15 with --audit-side-effects).

entries.name: paysponge/perplexity
entries.note: Catalog-listed endpoint returned http_200 at 2026-05-14 probe — non-402, agent_pay can't settle that URL. BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15, --audit-side-effects) discovered 3 SIBLING endpoints on the same host that DO return parseable Solana-USDC 402 challenges: /search ($0.01), /v1/agent ($0.01), /v1/async/sonar ($0.01). See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md for the parsed_ok evidence. These are payable today but NOT yet catalogued — they need per-endpoint description (request body shape, response shape verification) before catalog inclusion. Deferred to BAT-708. Until then, the agent should tell users that perplexity is listed upstream and the catalog URL itself doesn't pay, but BAT-708 will add searchable Perplexity endpoints to the catalog.

entries.name: paysponge/nyne
entries.note: Catalog-listed endpoint returns 402 amount=0 (free-from-x402's-perspective, refused by agent_pay design). BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15, --audit-side-effects) discovered 2 SIBLING endpoints on the same host that DO have demand and parse OK for Solana-USDC: /person/enrichment ($0.02) and /person/search ($0.02). See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md. These are payable today but NOT yet catalogued — person-enrichment/search is privacy-sensitive (PII lookup of third parties) and needs explicit safety scoping (authorized-use-only pattern, like 2captcha and textbelt-sms in catalog.json) before catalog inclusion. Deferred to BAT-708.

entries.name: merit-systems/stableenrich/enrichment
entries.note: v1 catalog declared POST /api/google-maps/place-details/partial but the captured 402 is GET (and the BAT-706 audit shows GET on that path returns http_400). We have NOT settled with POST + a real body to verify the curated method works. Demoted to unsupported in BAT-761 (PR #379 R7) — re-probe with POST in BAT-708 and re-promote with fresh capture if verified. Audit also found 32 sibling endpoints on the same host that are payable (see audit_pending); those are tracked separately for the Tier 2c catalog expansion (BAT-772).
